feed.title: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository
feed.subtitle: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository on GitHub

entry.title: [io.undertow:undertow-core] Undertow MadeYouReset HTTP/2 DDoS Vulnerability
entry.content:
A flaw was found in Undertow where malformed client requests can trigger server-side stream resets without triggering abuse counters. This issue, referred to as the "MadeYouReset" attack, allows malicious clients to induce excessive server workload by repeatedly causing server-side stream aborts. While not a protocol bug, this highlights a common implementation weakness that can be exploited to cause a denial of service (DoS).

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-9784
  • https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/CVE-2025-9784
  • https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2392306
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1778
  • https://issues.redhat.com/browse/UNDERTOW-2598
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1802
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1803
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1804
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1805
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-95h4-w6j8-2rp8

entry.title: [io.undertow:undertow-core] Undertow MadeYouReset HTTP/2 DDoS Vulnerability
entry.content:
A flaw was found in Undertow where malformed client requests can trigger server-side stream resets without triggering abuse counters. This issue, referred to as the "MadeYouReset" attack, allows malicious clients to induce excessive server workload by repeatedly causing server-side stream aborts. While not a protocol bug, this highlights a common implementation weakness that can be exploited to cause a denial of service (DoS).

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-9784
  • https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/CVE-2025-9784
  • https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2392306
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1778
  • https://issues.redhat.com/browse/UNDERTOW-2598
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1802
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1803
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1804
  • https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/pull/1805
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-95h4-w6j8-2rp8

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:publish-to-bitbucket] Jenkins Publish to Bitbucket Plugin is missing a permissions check
entry.content:
Jenkins Publish to Bitbucket Plugin 0.4 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified HTTP URL using attacker-specified credentials IDs obtained through another method, capturing credentials stored in Jenkins.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64150
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3576
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wpr5-rc2j-99p2

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:publish-to-bitbucket] Jenkins Publish to Bitbucket Plugin vulnerable to CSRF and missing permissions check
entry.content:
Jenkins Publish to Bitbucket Plugin 0.4 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified HTTP URL using attacker-specified credentials IDs obtained through another method, capturing credentials stored in Jenkins.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64149
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3576
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m244-6mff-p355

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:publish-to-bitbucket] Jenkins Publish to Bitbucket Plugin is missing a permissions check
entry.content:
Jenkins Publish to Bitbucket Plugin 0.4 and earlier does not perform a permission check in a method implementing form validation.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to enumerate credentials IDs of credentials stored in Jenkins. Those can be used as part of an attack to capture the credentials using another vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64148
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3570
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-v549-7pm5-f8qr

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:curseforge-publisher] Jenkins Curseforge Publisher Plugin does not mask API Keys displayed on the job configuration form
entry.content:
Jenkins Curseforge Publisher Plugin 1.0 and earlier stores API Keys unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These keys can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these keys, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64147
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3562
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-hv42-crpx-q355

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:nexus-task-runner] Jenkins Nexus Task Runner Plugin is missing a permission check
entry.content:
Jenkins Nexus Task Runner Plugin 0.9.2 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified URL using attacker-specified username and password.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64142
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3550
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h83r-7f9f-mqjj

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:nexus-task-runner] Jenkins Nexus Task Runner Plugin vulnerable to cross-site request forgery
entry.content:
Jenkins Nexus Task Runner Plugin 0.9.2 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified URL using attacker-specified username and password.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64141
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3550
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x2pv-fph3-phfx

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:themis] Jenkins Themis Plugin is missing a permission check
entry.content:
Jenkins Themis Plugin 1.4.1 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified URL.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64137
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3517
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jwm4-955w-4hj3

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:windocks-start-container] Jenkins Start Windocks Containers Plugin is missing a permission check
entry.content:
Jenkins Start Windocks Containers Plugin 1.4 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified URL.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64139
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3531
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mj6v-4wr4-gj57

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:jdepend] Jenkins JDepend Plugin vulnerable to XML external entity attacks
entry.content:
Jenkins JDepend Plugin 1.3.1 and earlier includes an outdated version of JDepend Maven Plugin that does not configure its XML parser to prevent XML external entity (XXE) attacks.

This allows attackers able to configure input files for the "Report JDepend" step to have Jenkins parse a crafted file that uses external entities for extraction of secrets from the Jenkins controller or server-side request forgery.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64134
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-2936
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jfg6-4gx3-3v7w

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:saml] Jenkins SAML Plugin does not implement a replay cache
entry.content:
Jenkins SAML Plugin 4.583.vc68232f7018a_ and earlier does not implement a replay cache.

This allows attackers able to obtain information about the SAML authentication flow between a user’s web browser and Jenkins to replay those requests, authenticating to Jenkins as that user.

SAML Plugin 4.583.585.v22ccc1139f55 implements a replay cache that rejects replayed requests.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64131
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3613
  • https://github.com/jenkinsci/saml-plugin/commit/6170b1013daf52770de29a66aeb57893aae1d7d6
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-j7r7-7qmf-xq87

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:azure-cli] Jenkins Azure CLI Plugin does not restrict the commands it executes
entry.content:
Jenkins Azure CLI Plugin 0.9 and earlier does not restrict which commands it executes on the Jenkins controller.

This allows attackers with Item/Configure permission to execute arbitrary shell commands on the Jenkins controller.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64140
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3538
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rh72-238f-g26q

entry.title: [com.openshift.jenkins:openshift-pipeline] Jenkins OpenShift Pipeline Plugin stores authorization tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files
entry.content:
Jenkins OpenShift Pipeline Plugin 1.0.57 and earlier stores authorization tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These token can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64143
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3553
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4653-9q2r-684q

entry.title: [io.jenkins.plugins:byteguard-build-actions] Jenkins ByteGuard Build Actions Plugin stores API tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files
entry.content:
Jenkins ByteGuard Build Actions Plugin 1.0 and earlier stores API tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These tokens can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these credentials, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64144
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3560
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2vmr-8c82-x8xq

entry.title: [io.jenkins.plugins:byteguard-build-actions] Jenkins ByteGuard Build Actions Plugin does not mask API tokens displayed on the job configuration form
entry.content:
Jenkins ByteGuard Build Actions Plugin 1.0 and earlier stores API tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These tokens can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these credentials, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64145
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3560
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vmm2-53rc-43v3

entry.title: [io.jenkins.plugins:eggplant-runner] Jenkins Eggplant Runner Plugin protection mechanism disabled
entry.content:
Jenkins Eggplant Runner Plugin 0.0.1.301.v963cffe8ddb_8 and earlier sets the Java system property jdk.http.auth.tunneling.disabledSchemes to an empty value as part of applying a proxy configuration.

This disables a protection mechanism of the Java runtime addressing CVE-2016-5597.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64135
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3326
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w5r3-gr8w-7fj5

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:themis] Jenkins Themis Plugin vulnerable to cross-site request forgery
entry.content:
Jenkins Themis Plugin 1.4.1 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified URL.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64136
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3517
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-93mh-mx9w-m69q

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:curseforge-publisher] Jenkins Curseforge Publisher Plugin stores API Keys unencrypted in job config.xml files
entry.content:
Jenkins Curseforge Publisher Plugin 1.0 and earlier stores API Keys unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These keys can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these keys, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64146
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3562
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-23vj-j6jc-w892

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:windocks-start-container] Jenkins Start Windocks Containers Plugin vulnerable to cross-site request forgery
entry.content:
Jenkins Start Windocks Containers Plugin 1.4 and earlier does not perform a permission check in an HTTP endpoint.

This allows attackers with Overall/Read permission to connect to an attacker-specified URL.

Additionally, this endpoint does not require POST requests, resulting in a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-64138
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-10-29/#SECURITY-3531
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6mgr-3374-4p3c
